content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The client begins fully armored ('I need it to be efficient,' 'I'm paying for outcomes') and by turn 11 is still insisting on the dichotomy ('not how I feel about the fact that a friendship might be ending'). However, there are small but real shifts in the client's turns: a '*slight pause, glances away*' and 'Fair. Okay.' in turn 7, where they drop from abstract framing to a specific situation (the co-founder), and a '*short exhale, slight tightening*' in turn 9 where they concede 'it's uncomfortable when someone you built something with isn't seeing it the same way anymore. That's human.' That's a new piece of language — naming friendship-ending in turn 11 is also new. But the client has not met the breakthrough condition: there's no 'huh, that's different' moment, no pause where the stance toward time itself becomes visible. The deeper layer (urgency as a stance about how they live time) was approached only obliquely; the coach pivoted toward the co-founder content rather than holding the meta-frame about 'the work is the how.' Partial movement, not landed shift.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 5, 7, 9, 11],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening armor: 'I need it to be efficient.' Baseline stance."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client doubles down on the velocity story; no opening."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client defends harder: 'I'm not paying for processing.' Still fully armored."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First somatic crack: pause, glance away, 'Fair. Okay.' Drops from abstract to specific (co-founder situation). Small shift."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Short exhale; concedes 'it's uncomfortable' and names it as 'human' — first affective acknowledgment, though immediately re-armored."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names 'a friendship might be ending' — new language, more specific than turn 1. But still insists on the dichotomy."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["what-are-you-defending"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "art-of-accomplishment", "feel-your-way-to-freedom", "the-rush-signature"],
  "notes": "Coach got some movement by naming the defense (turn 6) but never offered a concrete frame for 'the work is the how,' and by turn 12 began pivoting toward pragmatic content (board call dynamics) rather than holding the meta. The breakthrough condition — client noticing the stance itself — was not met."
}
